Andrej Karpathy \u2013 GitHub Repos.text:
**New this period:**
- nanochat repository, a ChatGPT model that can be purchased for $100
- karpathy's blog, updated on April 10, 2026
- autoresearch repository, for training AI agents on single-GPU nanochat
- jobs repository, a research tool for exploring Bureau of Labor Statistics data
- rustbpe repository, providing tiktoken training code in Rust
- cpython repository, a fork of the Python programming language
- hn-time-capsule repository, analyzing Hacker News discussions with LLMs
- llm-council repository, a council for answering questions with LLMs
- reader3 repository, an illustration of reading books with LLMs
- nanoGPT repository, for training and fine-tuning medium-sized GPTs

**Details:**
**nanochat**(https://github.com/karpathy/nanochat)
The nanochat repository is described as "The best ChatGPT that $100 can buy" and uses Python. It has 53,108 stars and was updated on May 5, 2026. 
The repository has 7,115 forks and is licensed under the MIT License. 
It appears to be a ChatGPT model that can be purchased for a relatively low cost. 

**autoresearch**(https://github.com/karpathy/autoresearch)
The autoresearch repository uses Python and has 79,711 stars. 
It was updated on March 26, 2026, and has 11,618 forks. 
The repository is for training AI agents on single-GPU nanochat training automatically.

**jobs**(https://github.com/karpathy/jobs)
The jobs repository uses HTML and has 1,631 stars. 
It was updated on March 16, 2026, and has 327 forks. 
The repository is a research tool for visually exploring Bureau of Labor Statistics Occupational Outlook Handbook data. 

**rustbpe**(https://github.com/karpathy/rustbpe)
The rustbpe repository uses Rust and has 448 stars. 
It was updated on January 3, 2026, and has 50 forks. 
The repository provides the missing tiktoken training code.

**cpython**(https://github.com/karpathy/cpython)
The cpython repository is a fork of the Python programming language and has 68 stars. 
It was updated on December 19, 2025, and has 7 forks. 

**hn-time-capsule**(https://github.com/karpathy/hn-time-capsule)
The hn-time-capsule repository uses Python and has 620 stars. 
It was updated on December 10, 2025, and has 61 forks. 
The repository analyzes Hacker News discussions from a decade ago in hindsight with LLMs.

**llm-council**(https://github.com/karpathy/llm-council)
The llm-council repository uses Python and has 18,476 stars. 
It was updated on November 22, 2025, and has 3,578 forks. 
The repository is for a council that works together to answer questions with LLMs.

**reader3**(https://github.com/karpathy/reader3)
The reader3 repository uses Python and has 3,597 stars. 
It was updated on November 18, 2025, and has 458 forks. 
The repository illustrates how to read books together with LLMs.

**nanoGPT**(https://github.com/karpathy/nanoGPT)
The nanoGPT repository uses Python and is for training and fine-tuning medium-sized GPTs.

Garry Tan \u2013 GitHub Repos.text:
**New this period:**
- Garry Tan's GitHub repositories, including gbrain, gstack, gbrain-evals, openclaw-render-template, openclaw, alphaclaw, resend_robot, Timber, and zurb-foundation-wysihtml5.

**Details:**
**garrytan's GitHub Repositories**(https://github.com/garrytan?tab=repositories)
Garry Tan's GitHub repositories include projects such as gbrain, an opinionated OpenClaw/Hermes Agent Brain, and gstack, a setup with 23 opinionated tools. The repositories are primarily written in TypeScript, with some using HTML, Dockerfile, JavaScript, and Ruby. The most recent updates were made on May 8, 2026, for gbrain and gstack.
